Part B.key_evidence: The central source is faintly point-like in the Science cutout, not clearly present at the same position in the Template, and appears as a compact positive residual in the Difference image rather than a broad artifact. ndethist is only 1 with deltajd 0, so this is a first isolated detection. The nearest PS1 source is 5.84 arcsec away and galaxy-like (sgscore1=0), so it is not a tight stellar counterpart at the alert position.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: This is most consistent with a real solar-system object (Asteroid class in this benchmark). The required low-ndethist check favors that: with only one historical detection, a compact point-like source in Science and no obvious coincident persistent source in Template is more naturally explained by a moving object than by a fixed astrophysical variable. The positive subtraction and stellar morphology in the alert extraction (classtar 0.98) are also compatible with a transient point source.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A possible alternative is a very young astrophysical transient such as a supernova on a very faint host, since the nearby PS1 source is faint and galaxy-like. However, the single detection only, lack of a clear host-centered persistent source at the candidate position, and no strong evidence for a stationary nuclear or stellar counterpart make that less supported than the mover interpretation.
